focus: RQ3: six-family mode/default matrix and declared-mode completeness across workflow assets and EXECUTION TARGETS
noveltyJustification: 4 of 5 findings were fully new corpus, matrix, or validation details and 1 converted the known reachability gap into concrete mutation acceptance criteria.
ruledOut: Treating target reachability as mode completeness

title: Live mode-pair corpus is complete but unenforced
summary: All 28 declared suffix-mode routers currently have both target rows and balanced on-disk auto/confirm assets.

title: Default resolution is a typed per-command policy
summary: Family behavior spans fixed confirm, input-completeness routing, ask, direct-route defaults, and command overrides.

title: Mode routes permit explicit many-to-one aliases
summary: SpecKit unattended aliases are distinct execution modes that intentionally reuse the auto YAML with extra metadata.

title: Reachability does not prove completeness
summary: Current checks resolve concrete targets but do not compare a declared-mode set to target rows and assets.

title: Completeness fixtures need invalid and valid topology mutations
summary: Missing assets, missing rows, wrong defaults, and orphans must fail; valid aliases and direct-route exemptions must pass.

acceptanceCriterion: All 35 routers encode topology, declared modes, normalized routes, aliases, and typed default resolution without a universal default.
Missing mode assets or EXECUTION TARGETS rows fail canonical command validation while valid many-to-one aliases pass.
Discovery covers all six families and rejects orphan mode assets without imposing pair assets on direct-route topologies.
Removing both a declared mode row and asset emits completeness failure independently of reachability.
Independent mutations cover missing asset, missing row, wrong default, orphan asset, valid alias, and direct-route exemption.
